title: Caregiver Support Network Community Outreach Team (CREST-CSN)
text:
Caregiver Support Network Community Outreach Team (CREST-CSN) - SupportGoWhere
Caregiver Support Network Community Outreach Team (CREST-CSN)
Share link
Supports caregivers of persons at risk of or with mental health conditions or dementia. They may have or are at risk of developing depression, anxiety and burn-out as a result of their caregiving responsibilities.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Free
Referral required
Collapse all sections
About this support

You can approach over 100 community mental health teams in the community islandwide such as CREST-CSN, providing the following support:

Increase public awareness of the challenges and stressors faced by caregivers and available resources for caregivers in the community through outreach events/activities.

Promote the identification of caregiver needs and link up to support services in the community.

Provide psychosocial intervention and support to caregivers’ mental well-being.

Empower caregivers to be able to support other caregivers




These community mental health teams are developed under the Community Mental Health Masterplan driven by the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC), together with the Ministry of Health (MOH). The teams are fully funded by the government and there is no out-of-pocket cost.

Who is this for?

Any individual who is:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Caregivers who are caring for persons at risk of or with mental health issues and/ or dementia

What to expect?

Applicants may need to fulfil the services’ in-take criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any.

How to apply?

Call or email the providers to make an appointment. Visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

If you are already seeing a General Practitioner (GP) or Polyclinic doctor, you may approach them for support.

Information last updated on 13 Mar 2026
